title: ⭐⭐ Revision (exam style): plans, comparisons and the way there

questions.prompt: Complete: "Skander ___ leave the hotel at nine o'clock to catch the early train."
questions.explanation: This is a fixed plan for one person (he), so we use "is going to" + base verb: is going to leave. "Are going to" does not agree with Skander, "going to" drops the verb be, and "is go to" is not correct.

questions.prompt: Complete: "London is ___ than Tunis: it has millions of people."
questions.explanation: "Big" is short (one vowel + one consonant), so we double the -g and add -er: bigger than. "Big" alone is not a comparison, "more big" marks it twice, and "biggest" is the superlative.

questions.prompt: Complete: "For Yosra, the Natural History Museum is ___ place in London."
questions.explanation: "Beautiful" is a long adjective, so its superlative is "the most beautiful" ✓. "The beautifulest" does not exist, "the more beautiful" is a comparative form, and "most beautiful" forgets the word "the".

questions.prompt: Someone asks the way. Complete: "How do I get to the station? — ___ straight on, then turn right."
questions.explanation: Directions use the base verb like an instruction: Go straight on. "Going", "to go" and "goes" are not used to tell someone the way — you simply say "go".

questions.prompt: Complete this suggestion: "___ we meet in front of Big Ben at noon?"
questions.explanation: "Shall we" + base verb is a natural way to suggest something: Shall we meet…? "Do we" and "Are we" ask ordinary questions, not a friendly suggestion, and "Let's" is not followed by "we" (it already means "let us").

questions.prompt: Complete: "___ Skander going to buy the red T-shirt or the blue one?"
questions.explanation: A "be going to" question about one person starts with "Is": Is Skander going to buy…? "Do" and "Does" belong to the present simple, and "Are" does not agree with one person.
